Tuesday.subject: Rachana Sharir
keyConcepts: Introduction to Marma points in the hand region.
Specific Marma points discussed include Kshipra, Talahridayam, Kurcha, Kurchashir, Indrabasti, Koorpara, Ani, Urvi, Lohitaksha, and Kakshadhara.
These Marma points are significant anatomical locations found in the hand and arm.
Students are assigned to prepare notes including diagrams for all the discussed Marma points.
Tuesday.transcript: Today we are going to discuss about the Marma points in our hand, which are Kshipra, Talahridayam, Kurcha, Kurchashir, Indrabasti, Koorpara, Ani, Urvi, Lohitaksha and Kakshadhara are the points which are found in the hands. All of you write and come this all the points with a diagram in notes. Submit me at 9 PM. And tomorrow and it's all very important for because the 14 marks from the Marma will be come in the Rachana 2, Rachana 2 question paper. All of you read and come it.
Tuesday.whatsappContext: Students are instructed to write down all the Marma points discussed, along with their diagrams, in their notes. The assignment must be submitted by 9 PM. All students should read and come prepared for tomorrow's class.
Tuesday.examAlert: Marma points are very important, as 14 marks related to them will appear in the Rachana 2 question paper.
